non_production_statement: C-DAG does not prove prevention or savings. It demonstrates how high-risk financial decisions can be made replayable, inspectable, and auditable before issues escalate.

cases.source_fact_summary: CFPB announced a $3.7B order tied to widespread mismanagement across auto loans, mortgages, and deposit accounts.
cases.failure_type: Consumer-harm and servicing-control breakdown across major retail product lines.
cases.affected_workflow: Auto-loan servicing, mortgage servicing, deposit-account operations, remediation escalation.
cases.likely_missing_evidence_artifact: Cross-workflow, replayable decision trace linking inputs, controls, and remediation actions.
cases.buyer_relevance: Demonstrates why enterprise governance teams need deterministic, decision-level evidence before issues become systemic.
cases.limitation_language: Reference mapping only. This does not prove C-DAG would have prevented losses or enforcement.

cases.source_fact_summary: Top fine categories included AML, misleading communications, trade reporting, and recordkeeping.
cases.failure_type: Control, supervision, and record-integrity gaps in regulated operational workflows.
cases.affected_workflow: Surveillance, communications review, trade reporting QA, recordkeeping governance.
cases.likely_missing_evidence_artifact: Tamper-evident control evidence connecting policy decisions and operational outcomes.
cases.buyer_relevance: Supports compliance and internal-audit teams that need portable evidence for supervisory reviews.
cases.limitation_language: Reference mapping only. This does not establish legal sufficiency for any specific rule.

cases.source_fact_summary: SEC officials highlighted continued focus on materially false or misleading AI-related claims.
cases.failure_type: Governance and disclosure mismatch between AI claims and control evidence.
cases.affected_workflow: Model-governance attestations, investor/compliance disclosures, risk committee oversight.
cases.likely_missing_evidence_artifact: Deterministic replay and trace-backed governance documentation for AI decision processes.
cases.buyer_relevance: Provides a concrete governance response pattern for AI-claim scrutiny and board-level challenge.
cases.limitation_language: Reference mapping only. This does not certify disclosure compliance.

cases.case_title: AI operational-loss research in U.S. BHCs
cases.source_fact_summary: Research reports higher operational losses per assets among BHCs with greater AI investment intensity.
cases.failure_type: Operational-risk exposure growth outpacing controls and governance maturity.
cases.affected_workflow: AI deployment governance, incident escalation, risk-adjusted change control.
cases.likely_missing_evidence_artifact: Pre-escalation decision evidence that can be replayed, challenged, and audited.
cases.buyer_relevance: Supports model-risk and operational-risk teams evaluating whether control depth matches AI scale.
cases.limitation_language: Reference mapping only. Correlation in public research is not causal proof for any institution.

cases.source_fact_summary: OCC highlighted technology adoption opportunities alongside risk-management expectations for banks.
cases.failure_type: Risk-governance gaps in model, cybersecurity, and compliance control execution.
cases.affected_workflow: Technology change governance, model risk, compliance control attestation, supervisory response.
cases.likely_missing_evidence_artifact: Integrated decision-level audit trail across model outputs, policy thresholds, and review steps.
cases.buyer_relevance: Maps C-DAG outputs to supervisory-exam readiness and model-risk committee evidence needs.
cases.limitation_language: Reference mapping only. This is not a substitute for regulatory examination standards.
